parsed.generic_mechanism: A coherent sequence that causally explains apparent reward, then reinterprets desire and failed restraint through that model, can turn escape from perceived loss into relief; scrambled dependencies prevent those same ingredients from accumulating.

paraphrased_evidence.A: Relevant causal ideas appear—self-created withdrawal, weak satiety, and learned reward associations—but sections arrive in a severely disordered sequence, so claims, examples, instructions, and conclusions cannot build persuasively.
paraphrased_evidence.B: The block advances from a withdrawal-relief account through manipulated appetite and conditioned preference to the deprivation loop of dieting, repeatedly reframing abstinence as release, though sweeping claims and analogies somewhat weaken credibility.

paraphrased_evidence.ours: Relevant causal ideas appear—self-created withdrawal, weak satiety, and learned reward associations—but sections arrive in a severely disordered sequence, so claims, examples, instructions, and conclusions cannot build persuasively.
paraphrased_evidence.ref: The block advances from a withdrawal-relief account through manipulated appetite and conditioned preference to the deprivation loop of dieting, repeatedly reframing abstinence as release, though sweeping claims and analogies somewhat weaken credibility.
